regions: Emerging markets (Eastern Europe, Middle East, Africa, Latin America, Asia)
Rest of world (Western Europe, Canada, Japan, Australia, New Zealand)

dependencies: Single source suppliers for certain raw materials and component parts
Reliance on certain suppliers without contractual arrangements (spot basis)
Dependence on resins and electromechanical devices
Concentration of manufacturing in specific facilities (e.g., North Cove facility for IV solutions)
Reliance on third-party sterilization facilities using ethylene oxide
risks: Supply chain disruptions from natural disasters (Hurricane Helene impact on North Cove facility)
Shortages of raw materials and component parts (resins, electromechanical devices)
Increased costs and higher transportation costs
Disruptions to ports and shipping routes
Conflict in the Middle East impact
Single source or sole source supplier risks
Difficulty qualifying new or alternative suppliers due to regulatory requirements
Inability to pass cost increases to customers due to contractual limits
Concentration risk from manufacturing at single facilities
Environmental regulatory changes affecting sterilization processes (ethylene oxide regulations)

regulatory_risks: FDA inspections and potential warning letters, product recalls, seizures, monetary sanctions, injunctions
European Union Medical Device Regulation compliance requirements (phased implementation through December 2028)
CE marking requirements for medical devices in European Economic Area
Regulatory approval delays or denials for new products
Good manufacturing practice compliance requirements
Adverse event reporting requirements
Environmental regulations (EPA regulation of ethylene oxide sterilization)
EEA ban on DEHP in immediate packaging of medicinal products
EEA phase-out of DEHP in medical devices by 2030
Potential restrictions on per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances and polyvinyl chloride
EEA prohibition on desflurane as inhalation anesthetic by 2026
Increased regulatory scrutiny on nitrosamine impurities
Anti-Kickback Statute compliance
False Claims Act compliance
FCPA (Foreign Corrupt Practices Act) compliance
Physician Payments Sunshine Act compliance
Healthcare reform and reimbursement policy changes
Price controls and government purchasing through public tenders
China volume-based procurement policies causing significant price cuts
EU sustainability regulations (EU Deforestation Regulation, EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive)
Export and import regulations, quotas, duties, tariffs
Trade policy changes and potential new tariffs (including 25% tariff on imports from Mexico and Canada)
HIPAA compliance
GDPR (EU General Data Protection Regulation) compliance
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) compliance
EU Data Act compliance
EU Artificial Intelligence Act compliance
NIS2 Directive (EU cybersecurity legislation) compliance
Pillar Two global minimum tax rate of 15% implementation
Climate change and sustainability disclosure requirements

sanction_risks: U.S. sanctions on certain countries affecting business operations
Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) restrictions
Bureau of Industry and Security export control restrictions
Sanctions related to war in Ukraine
Potential retaliation from trade tensions between U.S. and trading partners (including China)
Risk of government restrictions on foreign products in healthcare systems
Potential for escalation of conflicts affecting operations
